The '''''Republic''-class Star Destroyer''' was [[Walex Blissex/Legends|Walex Blissex's]] follow-up design to his [[Victory I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory''-class Star Destroyer]].<ref name="CTD" />

==Characteristics==
The ''Republic''-class Star Destroyer was manufactured by [[Rendili StarDrive/Legends|Rendili StarDrive]] for the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. It was advertised as a Rendili design owing to its architect, the former Rendili designer [[Walex Blissex/Legends|Walex Blissex]]. However, this was more of a political move designed to draw [[Rendili/Legends|Rendili]] away from the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], and the ship's bulbous, organic-like shape spoke of significant influence from the [[Mon Calamari Shipyards/Legends|Mon Calamari Shipyards]].<ref name="Warfare">''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]''</ref> The ''Republic''-class was smaller than the [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]], designed by Blissex's daughter [[Lira Wessex/Legends|Lira]], but was a much more efficient design.<ref name="CTD" />

Although the ''Republic''-class was only half the cost of an ''Imperial''-class and required one-fifth the crew size, it boasted 20 percent more firepower than an ''Imperial I''-class vessel. However, it was still outmatched by the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] and the various [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyers]].<ref name="CTD" />

Armament consisted of 40 heavy [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] batteries, 40 heavy turbolaser cannons, 20 [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]], and 10 [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] projectors. The ''Republic''-class was also equipped with a Class 2 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], the same as was found on the ''Imperial''-class.<ref name="CTD" />

The ''Republic''-class was only 1,250 meters long, significantly smaller than the 1,600-meter-long ''Imperial''-class. Because the smaller ship retained a similar level of weaponry, it had to sacrifice cargo space and endurance. The ''Republic''-class held enough [[Foodstuff/Legends|consumables]] to operate without resupply for 2 years, compared to 2 years for the ''Imperial I''-class and 6 years for the ''Imperial II''-class.<ref name="CTD" />

Only a single regiment of 3,200 ground troops could be carried aboard, as well as a single full New Republic wing. ''Republic''-class ships usually carried long-range [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]], rather than short-range fighters such as [[Defender starfighter|Defender]] and [[BTL-S8 K-wing assault starfighter|K-wing starfighters]].<ref name="CTD" />

==History==
[[File:Reptavian_ship.jpg|left|thumb|The [[Reptavian (starship)|''Reptavian'']], a ''Republic''-class Star Destroyer.]]
The ''Republic''-class was one of the early warship models developed for the New Republic's standardization programs. Hampered by a resurgence in fighting due to the [[Thrawn campaign]] in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]] and the [[Operation Shadow Hand|return]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] the [[10 ABY/Legends|following year]], production on the ''Republic''-class and other vessels was eventually restarted at a smaller level and the new ships were dispersed throughout the fleet.<ref name="CTD" />

The ''Republic''-class [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] was not incorporated into the [[New Class Modernization Program]], which chose instead to develop its largest ships from [[Republic Engineering Corporation]]'s [[Nebula-class Star Destroyer|''Nebula''-class Star Destroyer]] hull-design.
Despite this, by the time of the [[Black Fleet Crisis]], the New Republic had managed to build a considerable number of ''Republic''s.<ref name="SOL" />

==Behind the scenes==
The first official image of the ''Republic''-class Star Destroyer is found in ''[[Cracken's Threat Dossier]]''. The ship has many round features, a striking deviation from standard Star Destroyer designs. A subsequent depiction in [[Invasion: Revelations 3|''Invasion: Revelations'' 3]] confirmed this design.

In Part 12 of Jason Fry's endnotes for ''The Essential Guide to Warfare'', [[Paul R. Urquhart]] considered mentioning in the book that the ''Republic''-class Star Destroyer and the [[MC80B Star Cruiser]] were intended to be the same ship under different names, but it was ultimately cut from the final version.<ref name="endnotes 12">{{Tumblr|subdomain=jasonfry|url=post/25364458982/eg-to-warfare-endnotes-pt-12|name=Jason Fry's Dorkery|text=EG to Warfare: Endnotes Pt. 12}}</ref>
